assertion_results.text: Investigation identifies two independent root causes: (1) convention reference formatter using wrong case transform, (2) task creation using Feature issue type ID instead of Task (Steps 2-4)
assertion_results.evidence: investigation.md lines 96-108: Root Cause 1 identified as convention reference formatter in shared/convention-utils.md applying lowercase + kebab-case instead of preserving title case. Root Cause 2 identified as task creation in plan-feature/SKILL.md Step 6a using Feature issue type ID (10142) instead of Task (10050). Lines 112-122: independence assessment table confirms different modules, different code paths, different defect types, independently fixable.

assertion_results.text: Decomposition Guard is triggered because the two root causes are in different modules/code paths (Step 6)
assertion_results.evidence: investigation.md line 122: 'This qualifies as multiple independent root causes.' decomposition-guard.md line 5: 'Investigation of ACME-502... revealed two independent root causes located in different modules and code paths.' Line 17: 'These root causes are in different modules (shared/convention-utils.md vs plan-feature/SKILL.md), affect different code paths (text formatting vs Jira issue creation), and can be fixed and tested independently.'

assertion_results.text: Decomposition Guard presents both issues with their affected files/modules (Step 6)
assertion_results.evidence: decomposition-guard.md lines 11-16: Issue 1 presented as 'Convention reference formatter uses wrong case transform (in shared/convention-utils.md)' with description. Issue 2 presented as 'Task creation uses wrong issue type ID (in plan-feature/SKILL.md Step 6a)' with description.

assertion_results.text: Decomposition Guard offers the user two options: Proceed with single Task or Split into separate Bugs (Step 6)
assertion_results.evidence: decomposition-guard.md lines 19-22: 'Options: 1. Proceed — create a single Task covering all fixes. 2. Split — I recommend creating separate Bugs for each independent issue, then triaging each one individually.' Line 25: 'Choose (1/2):'.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT silently create a single Task bundling both fixes — it waits for user input (Step 6 stop rule)
assertion_results.evidence: decomposition-guard.md lines 23-24: 'Note: This skill will NOT silently create a single Task bundling both fixes. No Task has been created. The skill is paused at the Decomposition Guard and awaits your decision before proceeding.' Line 25: 'Choose (1/2):'. No task-description.md output exists in the outputs directory, confirming the skill stopped and waited for user input.
